input.headline: ‘My husband collects pictures of old men’s faces to give me’: Keira Knightley on art, ghosts and West Ham’s midfield

raw.headline: ‘My husband collects pictures of old men’s faces to give me’: Keira Knightley on art, ghosts and West Ham’s midfield

winner: I asked my husband why he was filling the hallway with portraits of elderly men, and he said it was for my protection because he wanted me to be able to see the ghosts of the West Ham midfield clearly.

candidates.text: I asked my husband for a portrait, and he handed me a frame filled with old men's faces — turns out, he was just trying to give me a gallery, not a ghost story.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Funny twist on the headline with good wordplay and specificity."}

candidates.text: I told my husband I wanted a portrait of him, and he handed me a box of old men's faces—turns out, he was just trying to make sure I never forget what a good looking guy I married.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the headline, specific reference to the husband's collection, and a touch of dry humor that aligns well with the context."}

candidates.text: My husband told me he collects pictures of old men's faces to give me as gifts, but I told him I’m not interested in a gallery of portraits—I’m just trying to find a way to tell him the West Ham midfield is the only thing in our house that's truly haunting.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a specific reference to West Ham midfield, ties humorously into the headline, and uses natural English with clever wordplay."}

candidates.text: I asked my husband why he was filling the hallway with portraits of elderly men, and he said it was for my protection because he wanted me to be able to see the ghosts of the West Ham midfield clearly.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.2, "reason": "Surprising and specific connection between the husband's collection and West Ham's midfield ghosts, with a touch of absurdity and natural flow."}

candidates.text: I asked my husband why he collects so many portraits of elderly men, and he said it’s just his way of keeping the West Ham midfield in perspective.

candidates.text: I asked my husband for a new album frame—he gave me a collage of his favorite ex-boyfriends. Said I’d like the "depth" he put into it.

candidates.text: What's the difference between Keira Knightley's husband collecting old men's faces and a detective solving a cold case? One involves more dramatic irony and fewer crime scene photos!

candidates.text: Keira Knightley's husband must have a *face* for collecting, because his collection of old men's faces is truly one in a *million*—I mean, he's got them on his wall!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke plays on the double meaning of 'face' and 'one in a million,' which is clever, but it's somewhat generic and doesn't fully tie into the specific context of Keira Knightley or her husband's collection as mentioned in the headline."}
